extraction.title: Free Vibrations of Ring- and Stringer-Stiffened Conical Shells

extraction_notes.note: Stage-1 manifest flag MF-001 stated Appendix Eq. (A4)/H_4 was MISSING between IMG-026 and IMG-027. During Stage-2 high-zoom reading this was found to be INCORRECT: the BOTTOM of the original IMG-026 contains H_4 / (A4) in full (it was cut off in the Stage-1 downsampled view). The user-supplied IMG-026B (which contains A4 and A5) was therefore corroborating, not gap-filling. The A4 text on IMG-026 and on IMG-026B AGREE verbatim, and the A5 text on IMG-026B and IMG-027 AGREE verbatim. (A4) below is therefore HIGH confidence with two independent sources. No real gap ever existed.

A comma in a subscript denotes partial differentiation w.r.t. the following variable(s) (paper: "The comma denotes differentiation with respect to the variable subscripts that follow it"). e.g. w,_ss = d^2 w/ds^2 ; u,_theta = du/dtheta ; w,_stheta = d^2 w/(ds dtheta).

"Starred" quantities (e.g. D_r*, S_st*, IN*7, m_r*) are placeholders in the Eq.(28) coefficient formulas whose concrete values depend on which modeling approach is used: the EQUIVALENT ORTHOTROPIC shell (Eqs. 15, 19 with A10/A11) or the GENERAL reinforced shell (Eqs. 17, 21 with A12). Star placement has been transcribed exactly and must be preserved (Stage 3 audit target).

IMG-026B has no on-disk PNG yet (it was supplied inline in chat). Its content was read from the supplied image. Stage 3's blind re-audit of IMG-026B will require that PNG to be saved at 00_inputs/images/batch_F/IMG-00 (26B).png. (A4/A5 are independently sourced from IMG-026/IMG-027, so extraction is not blocked.)

extraction.text: Strain-displacement relations are developed for a thin truncated circular conical shell and its reinforcing rings and stringers. Using these relations in an energy approach, methods are developed for determining the resonant frequencies of equally-spaced ring-stiffened, or ring-and-stringer- stiffened configurations. The stiffeners may be either widely separated or closely spaced. Computed values obtained using these methods are compared with the results of a series of experiments on ring-stiffened and ring-and-stringer-stiffened conical shells. Header codes "12.7, 12.3" and "Received 6 January 1969" appear at top. Authors O. E. Crenwelge, Jr. and D. Muster, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, University of Houston.

definitions.heading: LIST OF SYMBOLS (page 176) - transcribed from the paper; cross-consistent with notation_register.md
definitions.item: A = cross-sectional area, inches^2
D = extensional rigidity
E = modulus of elasticity, pounds per square inch
G = shear modulus, pounds per square inch
I = moment of inertia about centroidal axis, inches^4
J = torsional constant, inches^4
K = bending rigidity
P = number of rings
Q = number of stringers
S = rigidity moment
T = twisting rigidity; or kinetic energy
U_{mn}, V_{mn}, W_{mn} = amplitudes of generalized coordinates u_{mn}, v_{mn}, w_{mn} in the circumferential, meridional, and normal-to-shell directions, respectively
V = strain energy
b = distance between evenly spaced stiffeners, inches
c = distance from shell middle surface to stiffener centroid (positive when stiffener lies on outside of shell), inches
f = resonant frequency, cycles per second
g = acceleration of gravity, 386.4 in./sec^2
m = number of meridional half-waves; or mass per unit area or per unit length
n = number of circumferential waves
s = distance from cone apex along meridian to a point on shell middle surface, inches
s_r = distance of rth ring from cone apex in meridional direction, inches
s_1, s_2 = distance along meridian from cone apex to small base and to large base of conical shell, respectively, inches
t = time, seconds
t_w = thickness of thin shell wall, inches
u, v, w = displacements of point on shell middle surface in circumferential, meridional, and normal-to-shell directions, respectively
u_{mn}, v_{mn}, w_{mn} = time dependent generalized coordinates of displacements u, v, w, respectively
z = distance of a point in shell wall or in stiffeners from middle surface in direction normal to middle surface, inches
\alpha = angle between meridian on shell middle surface and large base of cone, radians
\beta = angle between evenly spaced stringers on circumference, radians
\gamma = specific weight, pounds per cubic inch
\epsilon = strain at distance z from shell middle surface
\bar{\epsilon} = membrane strains
\theta = angular distance in circumferential direction, radians
\theta_{st} = distance of st-th stringer from first stringer at theta_1 = 0 rad, radians
\nu_w = Poisson's ratio for shell wall material
\sigma = stress, pounds per square inch
\chi = change of curvature or twist
\omega = circular resonant frequency, radians/second
Subscripts w, r, st refer to shell wall, ring, and stringer, respectively
Subscripts s, theta, s-theta refer to meridional and circumferential directions and shear, respectively
definitions.notes: w is defined here as "normal-to-shell" but Eq.(25) text calls its direction "radial" (notation-register conflict NC008). T and m are overloaded (NC003/NC002). j conflicts with Table I geometric symbols (NC007).

extraction.text: Section: INTRODUCTION. Primarily a literature review (vibration of cylindrical, conical, stiffened and unstiffened shells; clamped/free and simply-supported conditions; energy and other methods) and a statement of scope. Not theory-bearing for the model itself. Modeling-relevant scope statement: the study develops strain-displacement relations and an energy method, computes natural frequencies and mode shapes for ring- and ring-and-stringer-stiffened truncated conical shells, and compares with experiment. No equations appear in the introduction. Full literature-review prose intentionally not transcribed (no modeling content). Flag none.

I. THEORY. "Consider a thin truncated conical shell of the geometry and coordinates shown in Fig. 1. The strains at a distance z from the shell middle surface are given by" [Eqs. 1-3]. Eqs. 4-6 give the extensional (membrane) strains in the meridional (s) and circumferential (theta) directions and the shear strain (s-theta); Eqs. 7-9 give the changes of curvature in s and theta and the twist.

equation.notes: Shell-wall meridional strain at distance z.
Shell-wall circumferential strain at distance z.
Shell-wall shear strain at distance z. Note factor 2 on the z*chi_stheta term (cf. factor 1 in Eqs 1-2).
Meridional membrane strain. v,_s = dv/ds.
Circumferential membrane strain.
Middle-surface shear strain.
Change of curvature, meridional.
Change of curvature, circumferential.
Twist. Term signs verified at high zoom: +, -, -, +.

extraction.text:
"These are the changes of curvature in the s and theta directions and the twist, respectively. The displacements of a point on the shell middle surface are denoted by u, v, w in the theta, s, z directions, respectively. The comma denotes differentiation with respect to the variable subscripts that follow it. It has been assumed that the shell displacements and displacement gradients are small so that linear shell theory is applicable." Then: "Consider a thin conical shell reinforced with rings and stringers. An element of this shell is shown in Fig. 2. Assuming that the stiffeners are thin, the interactions at the ring and stringer joints may be ignored, and the state of strain of a stiffener is normal strain in the direction of its largest dimension and twist; thus, we have for the rings" [Eqs 10-11] "and for the stringers" [Eq 12 ...].
(a) linear shell theory (small displacements/gradients); (b) stiffeners are thin -> ring/stringer joint interactions ignored; (c) stiffener state of strain = normal strain along its largest dimension + twist.

equation.notes: Ring circumferential strain at distance z.
Ring twist.
Stringer meridional strain at distance z. Equation number (12) read from IMG-007; the expression continues/closes on IMG-008's top region near Eq.(13).

Stringer twist (top line of IMG-008). This line is the most cramped on the page; the transcription mirrors the twist structure of Eq.(9). Confidence MEDIUM pending Stage-3 zoom confirmation of the exact denominators/signs - see AMB-001.

ambiguity.location: Eq. (13), top line of IMG-008 (stringer twist {}^{st}\chi_{s\theta}).
ambiguity.problem: The equation sits in a tightly-set top line with small stacked fractions; while it appears to mirror Eq.(9), the exact coefficients/denominators (e.g. the 2s denominator and the s^2 term) were not independently re-zoomed to the same certainty as Eq.(9).
ambiguity.possible_readings: Most likely identical in form to Eq.(9): w,_{s\theta}/(s cos a) - w,_\theta/(s^2 cos a) - u,_s tan a/(2s) + u tan a/s^2.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: Stage 3 blind audit to re-zoom IMG-008 top line and confirm each term/sign of Eq.(13).

extraction.text: Two modeling approaches are introduced. (1) EQUIVALENT ORTHOTROPIC shell: when stiffeners are closely/equally spaced their effects are distributed (smeared) over the shell surface; the shell is analyzed as an equivalent orthotropic shell (leads to Eqs. 14/15 and 18/19). (2) GENERAL reinforced shell (Ref. 15): the shell may have any number of stiffeners not necessarily closely or equally spaced; the effects are concentrated at the respective stiffener locations (leads to Eqs. 16/17 and 20/21).

equation.notes: Stringer circumferential spacing (unnumbered relation at top of IMG-010).

Strain energy, equivalent orthotropic (smeared) approach. Wall bracket coefficients verified at high zoom: membrane shear carries 1/2(1-nu_w); bending twist carries 2(1-nu_w). See SUSP-001 re: this 1/2-vs-2 asymmetry (deferred to Stage 5, NOT asserted as an error).
Rigidity/stiffness definitions for the equivalent orthotropic shell. "D_w and K_w are the same as before" is re-stated at Eq.(17). All divided by stiffener spacing b_r or b_st (smeared).

extraction.text: Fig. 1 (line diagram, lower-left of IMG-009). See FIG-001.

figure.caption: Thin truncated conical shell - geometry and coordinate system.
figure.labels: s_1, s_2, s (meridional coordinates); r; t_w (wall thickness); alpha (semi-vertex-related angle); r = s cos alpha; r_t = s cot alpha.
figure.notes: Defines the geometric relations r = s cos(alpha) and r_t = s cot(alpha). r_t meaning flagged "ambiguous" in notation_register (status carried, see AMB-002).

ambiguity.problem: The physical meaning of r_t is not stated on the figure; notation_register marks r_t as "ambiguous / physical meaning pending".
ambiguity.possible_readings: Likely the distance from the cone axis along the normal/horizontal projection (geometry helper), but not defined in visible text.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: Confirm whether any visible text defines r_t; otherwise leave as geometry helper.

equation.notes: Rigidity definitions for the GENERAL reinforced shell ("where D_w and K_w are the same as before"). Identical to Eq.(15) ring/stringer terms BUT WITHOUT division by b_r or b_st (concentrated, not smeared). This distinction is theory-critical.
Kinetic energy, equivalent orthotropic. Dots denote time derivatives. T here = kinetic energy (not twisting rigidity).

equation.eq: m_w = \gamma_w t_w/g, \qquad m_r = \gamma_r A_r/(g\,b_r), \qquad m_{st} = \gamma_{st} A_{st}/(g\,b_{st})
equation.notes: Mass-per-area terms, equivalent orthotropic (smeared; divided by spacing).
Kinetic energy, general reinforced shell. Same measure pattern as Eq.(16): ring -> s_r cos(alpha) d(theta); stringer -> ds.
Mass terms, general reinforced shell ("where m_w is the same as before"). No spacing division (concentrated).
Simply supported end conditions at both ends. Constrains u and w (not v) at s_1 and s_2.
Trial displacement, circumferential direction (after Grigolyuk, Ref.10). Note cos(n theta) and sin k_m(...).
Trial displacement, meridional direction. Note cos k_m(...) (unlike u and w which use sin k_m) and sin(n theta).
Trial displacement, "radial" direction (= normal-to-shell w per symbol list; wording conflict NC008).
Meridional wavenumber. m = number of meridional half-waves; n = number of circumferential waves.

equation.eq: u_{mn}=U_{mn}e^{j\omega t}, \qquad v_{mn}=V_{mn}e^{j\omega t}, \qquad w_{mn}=W_{mn}e^{j\omega t}
equation.notes: Harmonic time dependence of the generalized coordinates. j = sqrt(-1).

extraction.text: "The term s^2 is proportional to the cross-sectional area." (Paper's note immediately after Eq.27, explaining the s^2 factor in the assumed displacement fields.)

figure.caption: Element of reinforced conical shell.
figure.labels: STRINGER, THIN WALL, RING; coordinate axes z, s, theta shown.

figure.caption: Experimental configurations.
figure.labels: Cross-section sketch labels: m, w, h, k, beta. Elevation/assembly labels: d, D, b_r/2, b_r, M=(s_2 - s_1), section A-A, callouts "1/4 in. ALUMINUM END PLATE", "ADJUSTABLE NUTS", "1/2 in. THREADED STEEL ROD (20 THREADS/IN.)".
figure.notes: The tiny cross-section labels (m, w, h, k) coincide with notation conflicts NC005/NC006 (geometric vs displacement/area symbols). See AMB-003. The relation M = (s_2 - s_1) is confirmed.

ambiguity.location: Fig. 3 cross-section dimension labels m, w, h, k (and section marker A).
ambiguity.problem: These single-letter labels are very small and collide with other meanings: m (modal/mass), w (displacement), A (area). The exact geometric dimension each denotes for the stiffener cross-section is not spelled out in visible text; Table I lists numeric values for j, h, m, k as geometric dimensions but does not draw them.
ambiguity.possible_readings: They are stiffener cross-section dimensions (width/height/leg sizes) consistent with Table I rows j, h, m, k; precise mapping to physical edges is not legible.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: User/Stage-4 confirmation of the geometric meaning of Fig. 3 / Table I dimensions j, h, m, k (and w) for the stiffener sections; do not guess.

extraction.text: "The equations of motion for the vibrating shell can be expressed in the matrix equation" [Eq. 28] "where the H_i are given in the Appendix. The characteristic equation of the matrix Eq. 28 is" [Eq. 29] "where" [Eq. 30]. After Eq.30: "Of the three natural frequencies obtained from Eq. 29 for each combination of m and n, only the lowest (which is associated with radial motion of the shell) is of interest." Preceding text also notes the s^2 factor shifts the point of maximum displacement toward the large end of the cone (stiffness decreases with increasing radius).

equation.eq: \begin{bmatrix} H_1-\omega^2 H_7 & H_2 & H_3 \\ H_2 & H_4-\omega^2 H_8 & H_5 \\ H_3 & H_5 & H_6-\omega^2 H_9 \end{bmatrix} \begin{Bmatrix} u_{mn} \\ v_{mn} \\ w_{mn} \end{Bmatrix} = \begin{Bmatrix} 0 \\ 0 \\ 0 \end{Bmatrix}
equation.notes: Symmetric 3x3 generalized eigenproblem. Diagonal mass-like terms multiply omega^2 (H_7,H_8,H_9).
Characteristic cubic in X. Alternating signs verified. X defined in Eq.(30).
All inner signs of A_0 verified at high zoom: +H1H4H6 +2H2H3H5 -H2^2H6 -H3^2H4 -H1H5^2, the whole negated, over (H7H8H9).
Generalized-displacement (mode ratio) relation for u_mn.

equation.eq: v_{mn} = \left\{ \big[ (H_1 - \omega^2 H_7)(H_6 - \omega^2 H_9) - H_3^2 \big] \big/ \big[ H_2 H_3 - H_5(H_1 - \omega^2 H_7) \big] \right\} w_{mn}
equation.notes: Mode ratio for v_mn in terms of w_mn. Nested brackets/grouping verified.
Normalization of the radial generalized coordinate.

table.caption: Shell dimensions.
table.columns: Symbol(unit) | Shell No. 1 | Shell No. 2 | Shell No. 3
table.notes: Symbols j, h, m, k here are GEOMETRIC stiffener dimensions (notation conflicts NC002/NC007); their drawn meaning is in Fig.3 but not legible (AMB-003). Blanks for Shell 1 reflect Q=0 (no stringers).

extraction.text: III. RESULTS AND DISCUSSION (modeling-relevant points only; plotted data NOT digitized per instruction): - For each natural frequency, only the lowest root (radial motion) is of physical interest. - The EQUIVALENT ORTHOTROPIC shell theory is reported as inadequate for shells with a few widely separated sturdy stringers for m > 1 (it does not predict the double natural frequencies observed). - The GENERAL theory accounts for two natural frequencies when n is a multiple/submultiple of the number of stringers; for n = 3, 6, 9 two natural frequencies can be computed for each mode depending on whether all stringers lie on nodes or all on antinodes (observed experimentally). - As n increases, the meridional response shortens and shifts toward the large end of the shell (Weingarten effect), explaining computed/measured differences at large n. These qualitative statements bear on which approach to implement and on multiplicity of roots.

figure.caption: Conical shell reinforced with three rings and six stringers.
figure.notes: Photograph (Shell No. 3). No theory data; caption only per instruction.

figure.caption: Comparison of theory (general reinforced shell) with experiment (Shell No. 1).
figure.labels: Axes: Frequency (kHz) vs Number of Circumferential Waves n; legend keyed by number of meridional half-waves (theory line vs experiment markers).
figure.notes: Frequency-comparison plot. Per user directive, plotted values NOT digitized/analyzed.

figure.caption: Comparison of theory (general reinforced shell) with experiment (Shell No. 2).
figure.notes: Left plot on IMG-022. Not digitized.

figure.caption: Comparison of theory (general reinforced shell) with experiment (Shell No. 3).
figure.notes: Right plot on IMG-022. Not digitized.

figure.caption: Example of nodal pattern for mode of vibration.
figure.labels: Developed sector map; dashed = node line; X = shaker location (NOT eigenvalue X, conflict NC004); r = ring, st = stringer; annotated Shell 1, m = 1 or 2, n = 7 or 6, f = 3514 cps.

figure.caption: Example of nodal pattern for mode of vibration.
figure.labels: Developed sector map; dashed = node line; X = shaker location; r = ring, st = stringer; annotated Shell 3, m = 1, n = 9, f = 5090 cps.

missing.what: Figures 6, 8, 10 (equivalent-orthotropic comparison plots for Shells 1, 2, 3) are referenced in text but not present in the image set.
missing.impact: None for theory extraction (plots, not equations). Recorded for completeness; per user directive these are not required.

extraction.text: Appendix A. Components in the Frequency Matrix of Eq. 28. The H_i (i=1..9) below are expressed via the integral/summation shorthand quantities IN1..IN16 (and their starred variants IN*1..IN*16). Starred quantities take values per the chosen approach (see N-003).

equation.notes: H_1. cos exponents verified line-by-line: terms 1-2 /cos a; S_r* term /cos^2 a; K_w,K_r* terms /cos^3 a; last two /cos a. Starred terms exact.
H_2. All four leading signs verified (-,-,-,+ then -). Linear in n.

H_3. Note the S_r* bracket uses (2 - (n^2+sin^2 a)/cos^2 a) whereas the K_w/K_r* brackets use (2 - n^2/cos^2 a). Last three lines verified from IMG-026 lower region; see AMB-004 for one denominator-power check.

ambiguity.location: Eq. (A3), the nu_w K_w term and the (1-nu_w)/2 K_w term (third-from-last and second-from-last lines).
ambiguity.problem: These two lines sit at the page foot just above H_4; the sin(alpha)/cos(alpha) prefactor power and the "2 k_m sin a / cos a" coefficient were read at moderate zoom and could carry a small mis-read in the cos-power or the leading numeric (2 vs other).
ambiguity.possible_readings: As transcribed: nu_w K_w (pi sin a/cos a)[2(IN6)+4k_m(IN12)-k_m^2(IN8)]n and -(1-nu_w)/2 K_w (2 k_m sin a/cos a)[(IN12)+k_m(IN2)]n.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: Stage 3 blind re-zoom of IMG-026 foot lines to confirm cos powers and the 2k_m coefficient.

equation.notes: H_4. SOURCED FROM TWO IMAGES THAT AGREE VERBATIM: the bottom of original IMG-026 and the user-supplied IMG-026B. (Resolves the Stage-1 MF-001 false alarm - see N-001.)
H_5. SOURCED FROM TWO IMAGES THAT AGREE VERBATIM: IMG-027 (top) and user-supplied IMG-026B (bottom). Note the k_m^3(IN9) cubic term and the {...}(IN*15) grouping.

H_6 (longest component). Verified at high zoom: squared brackets (2 - n^2/cos^2 a)^2 in the K_w and K_r* lines; k_m^4 top exponents in the two brace-groups; the nu_w K_w cross-multiplied brace. Confidence MEDIUM overall only because of the term count/density; no specific sub-term currently reads as wrong. See AMB-005.

ambiguity.location: Eq. (A6), H_6 - the two large brace-groups (K_w{...} and K_st*{...}) and the nu_w K_w cross-product brace.
ambiguity.problem: Extremely dense; while each visible symbol was read, the sheer number of nested signs/exponents (k_m^2,k_m^3,k_m^4; the -4k_m^2[(IN8)-4(IN2)] inner bracket; the cross-multiplied (2 - n^2/cos^2 a)) warrants an independent second read.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: Stage 3 blind audit to re-zoom each line of H_6 on IMG-027 and confirm every exponent/sign, especially the inner bracket -4k_m^2[(IN8)-4(IN2)] and the trailing +4k_m^2(IN2)-k_m^3(IN14) in the nu_w K_w brace.

equation.notes: H_7. Ends in (IN9)(IN*16).
H_8. Ends in (IN3)(IN*15).
H_9. Ends in (IN9)(IN*15). NB: H_7 and H_9 differ ONLY in the last factor (IN*16 vs IN*15) - verified.

extraction.text: "In these equations, the proper values must be substituted for the starred (*) terms in the coefficient equations, depending upon which of the two approaches is used. For the equivalent orthotropic shell, the values of D_r, S_r, K_r, T_r, m_r used in the components of Eq. 28 (as defined above) are given in Eqs. 15 and 19; however, for this case the stringer properties are now" [Eq. A10].

equation.eq: \begin{aligned} D_{st} &= E_{st}A_{st}/((\cos\alpha)\beta),\\ S_{st} &= E_{st}A_{st}c_{st}/((\cos\alpha)\beta),\\ K_{st} &= E_{st}(I_{st}+A_{st}c_{st}^2)/((\cos\alpha)\beta),\\ T_{st} &= G_{st}J_{st}/((\cos\alpha)\beta),\\ m_{st} &= \gamma_{st}A_{st}/(g\beta\cos\alpha). \end{aligned}
equation.notes: Stringer properties for the equivalent orthotropic shell. Denominator is (cos a) beta = beta cos(alpha), WITHOUT an explicit s factor (contrast Eq.15 where b_st = s beta cos a). See SUSP-002 (deferred to Stage 5; transcribed faithfully, not "corrected").

extraction.text: "The values of (IN1)-(IN16) for the equivalent orthotropic shell are" [Eq. A11 block follows]. (The block is labeled (A11) per IMG-030's back-reference "obtained from Eqs. A11".)

equation.notes: Equivalent-orthotropic IN definitions. IN1-IN4 (with + sign on the k_m terms for IN3/IN4) vs IN7-IN10 (with - sign and +k_m^4) are the matched cos/sin-pair integrals. k_m^4 exponents in IN3,IN4,IN9,IN10 verified at high zoom. (IN16) for this case is NOT visible - see MISS-002.

missing.what: The equivalent-orthotropic definition of (IN16) is not visible. IMG-029 ends at "(IN15) = pi," followed by "and"; IMG-030 then jumps to the GENERAL case ("For the general case ..."). The single line "(IN16) = ..." for the equivalent orthotropic shell falls in the un-imaged gap.
missing.most_probable_value: By the pattern (IN15 = pi is the circumferential integral of sin^2/cos^2 n*theta over 0..2pi), (IN16) = pi is strongly implied for the equivalent orthotropic shell - but this is NOT asserted as extracted; it is flagged for resolution.
missing.impact: (IN16) appears in H_1, H_4, H_6, H_7, H_9. A missing equivalent-orthotropic value would block that approach's implementation; the general-case (IN16) IS given in Eq. A12.
missing.needed_resolution: User to supply the IMG-029->IMG-030 gap line, or confirm (IN16) = pi for the equivalent orthotropic shell.

extraction.text: "For the general case of reinforced conical shells, the stiffener properties are given by Eqs. 17 and 21, and the values of IN1-IN16 are obtained from Eqs. A11 with the following exceptions, for the starred terms" [Eq. A12 list]. (I.e. for the GENERAL approach, the non-starred IN keep their A11 polynomial forms; the STARRED terms IN*2, IN*4, IN*6, IN*7, IN*8, IN*10, IN*12, IN*13, IN*15, IN*16 take the summation forms below.)

equation.eq: \begin{aligned} (IN2) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^3 \cos^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN4) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^5 \cos^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN6) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r \sin^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN7) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^2 \sin^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN8) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^3 \sin^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN10) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^5 \sin^2 k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN12) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^2 \sin k_m(s_r-s_1)\cos k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN13) &= \sum_{r=1}^{P} s_r^3 \sin k_m(s_r-s_1)\cos k_m(s_r-s_1),\\ (IN15) &= \sum_{st=1}^{Q} \sin^2 n\theta_{st} \quad\text{or}\quad (IN15) = \sum_{st=1}^{Q} \cos^2 n\theta_{st},\\ (IN16) &= \sum_{st=1}^{Q} \cos^2 n\theta_{st} \quad\text{or}\quad (IN16) = \sum_{st=1}^{Q} \sin^2 n\theta_{st}, \end{aligned}
equation.notes: General reinforced-shell exception values for the starred IN terms (ring sums over r=1..P; stringer sums over st=1..Q). The "or" pairs for IN15/IN16 let the analyst orient stringers on nodes vs antinodes (theta_1 = 0). Labels printed as (IN2)... but per the heading these define the starred terms IN*2 etc. (see AMB-006). The non-listed IN (1,3,5,9,11,14) retain their A11 polynomial forms.

ambiguity.problem: The summation definitions are printed with unstarred labels "(IN2)", "(IN4)", ... yet the introducing sentence says they are the values "for the starred terms". So these define IN*2, IN*4, ... in the GENERAL approach, while the same H-formulas also contain unstarred IN2, IN4, ... (which keep A11 forms). The star/no-star bookkeeping is implicit, not typographically explicit, in this list.
ambiguity.possible_readings: (a) These ARE the starred-term (IN*k) values for the general case (most consistent with the heading "for the starred terms"); (b) literal unstarred reading (less consistent). Reading (a) is strongly preferred by context.
ambiguity.needed_resolution: Stage 5 to fix the convention: in the general approach, starred IN*k = the A12 summation forms; unstarred INk = the A11 polynomial forms. Confirm no H-formula intends an unstarred INk to take a summation form.

extraction.text:
Closing appendix text (key global assumptions): "where theta_1 = 0. In this way, it is possible to orient the stringers on the shell with respect to nodes and antinodes, thereby changing the values of strain and kinetic energies and the resonant frequency for a particular nodal pattern. In Eqs. A1-A8, it is assumed that all rings have the same material and cross-sectional properties and all stringers have the same material and cross-sectional properties; however, the materials of the shell, rings, and stringers may be different. Rotatory inertia, transverse shear, and structural damping are neglected."
(a) theta_1 = 0 (first stringer reference angle); (b) all rings identical (material + section); all stringers identical (material + section); shell/ring/stringer materials may differ; (c) NEGLECTED: rotatory inertia, transverse shear, structural damping.

suspected_issue.location: Eq.(14) and Eq.(16) wall strain-energy bracket: membrane shear term coefficient vs bending twist term coefficient.
suspected_issue.observation: The membrane part carries (1/2)(1-nu_w) on epsilon_bar_{s theta}^2, while the bending part carries 2(1-nu_w) on chi_{s theta}^2. This 1/2-vs-2 asymmetry is notable.
suspected_issue.why_it_may_matter: If both were meant to represent the same (1-nu_w)-type shear/twist energy weighting, one coefficient could be a typo. However, it can also be CORRECT given the factor-2 in Eq.(3) (wall shear strain uses -2 z chi_{s theta}) and the standard plate energy bookkeeping where the twist energy naturally carries a different numeric factor.
suspected_issue.most_defensible_interpretation: Transcription is faithful to the page; likely intentional and consistent with Eq.(3)'s factor 2. Defer to Stage 5 dimensional/energy check; do NOT alter.

suspected_issue.location: Eq.(A10) equivalent-orthotropic stringer properties denominator (cos a) beta, compared with Eq.(15) D_st = E_st A_st/b_st and b_st = s beta cos a (Eq. U01).
suspected_issue.observation: Eq.(15) smears stringer stiffness over spacing b_st = s*beta*cos(alpha) (s-dependent). Eq.(A10) writes the same properties over (cos a)*beta (NO explicit s). The two are inconsistent unless an s factor is absorbed elsewhere (e.g. via the s-weighting inside the IN integrals or a deliberate redefinition for the Eq.28 components).
suspected_issue.why_it_may_matter: An accidental dropped s would change the dimensional scaling of the smeared stringer terms and hence the predicted frequencies.
suspected_issue.most_defensible_interpretation: Transcribed faithfully as (cos a) beta. Likely the s-dependence is handled by the s-power IN integrals so the per-unit-area smearing here is by the angular spacing beta*cos(alpha) at the local radius - but this must be reconciled in Stage 5. Do NOT "fix".

coverage_summary.appendix_equations: (A1)-(A12) all extracted. A1-A5,A7-A9 HIGH; A6 MEDIUM/AMB-005; A4/A5 double-sourced.
coverage_summary.unnumbered_equations: b_st = s beta cos a (EQ-U01) extracted.
coverage_summary.tables: Table I (TBL-I) and Table II (TBL-II) fully extracted.
coverage_summary.figures: Fig.1-5,7,9,11,12 captured (captions/labels). Plots not digitized per directive. Fig.6,8,10 absent (MISS-001).
coverage_summary.definitions: Full List of Symbols (DEF-SYMBOLS) and rigidity/mass/IN definitions extracted.
coverage_summary.assumptions: Linear shell theory; thin stiffeners (joint interactions ignored); simply-supported ends; identical rings / identical stringers; rotatory inertia, transverse shear, structural damping neglected; theta_1=0.
coverage_summary.preliminary_suspected_issues: SUSP-001 (1/2-vs-2 shear/twist coefficient), SUSP-002 (A10 missing s in denominator).
coverage_summary.prohibitions_honored: No CSV used; no code written; plots not digitized; nothing guessed/silently corrected.
